campaigns.title: Help Chitra save disabled children from poor families from a life of neglect and abuse
campaigns.description: Chitra Shah noticed that a patient with disabilities had missed several of her appointments with a psychologist, where she worked. Determined to find out why, Chitra went to the girl’s house which was located near a garbage dump, and she found the house locked. But she kept hearing noises from within the house. Looking inside, she found the missing patient tied to a chair and covered in her own urine and waste. The patient was a young girl with Down’s Syndrome and because of her disability; she was locked up every day and suffered in silence. This incident led Chitra Shah to establish Satya Special School in 2003. Since then, she has provided thousands of special needs children with therapies, education and vocational skills training to live their lives with dignity and respect. Many children with disabilities are abandoned because of their disabilities. Satya Special School believes in empowering disabled children and helping them stand on their own independently so they never have to be shunned, humiliated and abused because of their disabilities. Your support can help children with disabilities receive therapy and education. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India), & 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (USA). Place of rehabilitation and transformation For 20 years, Satya Special School has been working with special-needs children in 10 centres located in rural and urban Puducherry. The centres have trained teachers and physical therapists who work with the children to identify their special needs and develop physical and educational programs to help them be integrated into mainstream life. The children are provided with multiple therapies such as physiotherapy, hydrotherapy, speech therapy, art therapy and more to improve their physical condition and help them function independently. The school also provides the children with specialized educational support to help them integrate into mainstream schools. This School Readiness Program includes one-on-one special education, activity-based learning, visual media learning and more. Satya Special School is also equipped with facilities such as a sensory walkway, an outdoor inclusive disabled-friendly play park, a wheelchair accessible vehicle and many more facilities to assist children with physical disabilities.Need for the fundraiserThe work of Satya Special School is making an impact in the lives of the children, many of whom have learned to function independently, been enrolled in mainstream schools and secured employment. But the school is in desperate need of funds to afford rehabilitation equipment such as walkers and standing frames, custom made shoes for people with disabilities and other therapeutic interventions. Your support will help the special needs children continue to have access to the tools, resources and therapies that make their lives better and give them a chance to have self-reliant and hopeful lives.The parents and guardians of the children were previously in despair regarding their children’s helplessness but because of Satya Special School, they are watching their children grow into independent and capable individuals who work towards their dreams for the future. The stigma, discrimination and abuse of disabled children is widespread, and many special-needs children are abandoned by their own families. But ”we transform their lives through building their abilities” , shares Chitra Shah, the founder of Satya Special School. Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support Satya Special School in their mission to provide multiple therapies, rehabilitation, academic support and future job readiness skills to 61 special needs children.

campaigns.title: Help Leena in saving girls from being lost forever in red-light areas
campaigns.description: "Hundreds of young girls are lost forever in the dark lanes of red-light areas," says Leena Kejriwal, the founder of Missing Link Trust. These girls endure unimaginable abuse and exploitation, being lured or trafficked into an industry that breaks them physically and emotionally. Missing Link Trust is on a mission to prevent the tragedy before it happens, and also rehabilitate girls and women who are rescued from forced sex work.Leena’s journey began with her powerful street art project in Kolkata, where she painted haunting silhouettes of missing girls to raise awareness against sex trafficking. Now, she leads a larger fight — one that focuses on prevention and rehabilitation. But this mission needs your support.You can save countless girl children from going missing forever. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Preventing Trafficking Before It Begins“When girls come into a red-light area, the first thing is that they are broken, physically and emotionally,” Leena says. “It’s like they will be caught, hands and legs tied, and all heinous crimes committed on them.” These are the brutal realities that Missing Link Trust fights against every day.Many of these girls are from impoverished regions like the Sundarbans, and traffickers prey on their vulnerability. “It’s shocking that they sell themselves for as low as a hundred rupees,” Leena adds. "They ready her through violent acts, removing all threads of shame from her."But why wait until a girl is trafficked to save her? Missing Link Trust believes in stopping trafficking at its root — through “prevention, prevention, prevention” as Leena puts it. The goal is simple: protect girls from ever being lost to this darkness.A Safe World for Children“Our mission is the prevention of abuse and exploitation of children,” Leena emphasizes. “Our vision is to create a safe world for children, and we want to do this with a preventive methodology.”Every Donation MattersYour support will help Missing Link Trust continue its vital work of rescuing, rehabilitating, and preventing the trafficking of young girls. The funds raised will go directly towards:Prevention programs in vulnerable areas to stop trafficking before it happens.Rehabilitation services for girls rescued from red-light areas, providing them with medical care, emotional support, and education.Awareness campaigns to reach at-risk communities and ensure girls are protected from traffickers.Together, We Can Make a Difference“Why wait for a girl to get trafficked to save her?” asks Leena. With your help, we can stop this horror before it begins. Your donation will help us create a world where no girl is lost to the nightmare of trafficking.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest and most trusted online giving platform, to support Missing Link Trust to save young girls from being trafficked and help rehabilitate those rescued.

campaigns.title: Help Velugu rescue the abandoned elderly mothers left to beg on the streets and care for them. Donate now
campaigns.description: Velugu, means "light" in Telugu, and the Velugu Old Age Home has been a true beacon of hope for scores of abandoned elderly women. Since 2000, the Velugu Old Age Home has provided more than just shelter. It offers a loving family, dignity, and compassionate care to its residents who have nowhere else to turn. However, rising costs and limited resources now threaten this vital sanctuary. Many more elderly women remain on waiting lists, desperate for support. Donate nowThis fundraiser on the give.do platform aims to ensure Velugu’s light continues to shine brightly. Your donation will directly provide nutritious meals, essential medicines, safe housing, and emotional support for those who need it most. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).“It is heartbreaking to see mothers getting abandoned by their own sons after either the mother has transferred her property’s share or when she doesn’t have anything to offer to her child in the form of gold or pension money,” says Uday Mohan Reddy, Secretary, Velugu Old Age Home.Over the years, Velugu has helped scores of elderly women find a safe haven to spend their final years. Away from the pain and abuse they may have gone through before coming here. Many women were found on the streets, sometimes begging.“Many of these elderly women lived their lives in middle class families and had decent lifestyles. After their husbands died or when they became very old, their children started treating them as a burden and started to abuse them. They were left with no other choice but to leave their homes. Some were pushed out,”: says Bhagya Lakshmi, Convenor, Velugu.By donating through give.do, you become part of this meaningful mission. You can help extend Velugu’s reach, brightening the lives of vulnerable seniors and ensuring no one is left in the dark. For over two decades, this sanctuary has offered more than just shelter—it has provided a loving home to over 85 elderly women, most of whom are widows abandoned by their families. The home offers nutritious meals, clean clothing, basic medical attention, and emotional support, ensuring that its residents spend their golden years in an environment of respect and warmth.However, this noble mission lies a pressing challenge: resources are stretched thin. Despite the dedication of its caretakers and staff, Velugu is unable to extend support to all the elderly in need due to financial constraints. Rising costs of essential supplies, medicines, and utilities have further intensified the struggle. It is here that the power of collective generosity can make a transformative difference.Every contribution, no matter the size, brings Velugu closer to its goal of leaving no senior behind. Donations will help admit those on the waiting list, upgrade medical facilities, and ensure that residents receive not just survival-oriented support, but also opportunities for joy and engagement. The give.do platform also offers donors transparency and trust, providing updates on how their contributions are making a difference. This creates a meaningful connection between supporters and the beneficiaries, turning empathy into action.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Velugu Old Age Home in its mission to provide a loving home to abandoned elderly mothers.

campaigns.title: Help Mangaltai give 150 abandoned HIV+ orphans life-saving care, education and a loving home
campaigns.description: “During a visit to a nearby village, I found two abandoned babies left to die in a cowshed. Visibly fragile and covered in dirt, the condition of the little ones left me in shock. Moved by the sight, I inquired in the area and was told that these babies were left there by their own family after they were diagnosed with HIV. I immediately brought the babies back home. This was in 2001 and that marked the beginning of a two-decade-long struggle to save abandoned HIV+ children from dying on the streets,” says 75-year-old Mangal Shah, fondly addressed as tai by her 150 adopted grandchildren. Mangaltai founded Palawi with a mission to give abandoned HIV+ children a second chance at life and, more importantly, to right the wrongs that society had done to them. For over two decades, she has rescued hundreds of children off the streets and given them the love they were denied for no fault of theirs. She has given the children life-saving medical care and education while helping them lead normal lives.“It does not cost us money to give them the love they deserve, but the cost of medical care, food, shelter, and education is more than what I can shoulder on my own and I need urgent help,” pleads Mangaltai. Help 70-year-old Mangaltai save the lives of her 150 HIV+ grandchildren. Donate now. Every child deserves a futureHailing from the predominantly rural area of Pandharpur in Maharashtra, Mangaltai faced countless challenges and opposition from people in the area. “I was often told that these children are HIV+ and they are not going to survive long. What is the point of wasting money, time and effort behind them,” recalls Mangaltai. “But I told everyone I am not going to give up on any of these children. Every child deserves love and care. Every child deserves to have a chance at a future,” she adds. Narrating a story about a 5-year-old boy Mangaltai says, “We rescued 5-year-old Pranay (name changed) from a nearby village in 2005. He was severely malnourished, sick and had a gaping wound on his head. Seeing the baby’s condition and the maggot-infested wound, doctors gave him six months to live. But Pranay is now 22 years old, he is financially independent and leading a normal life with dignity. What would have happened if I gave up on him?” Mangaltai has faced severe discrimination all along her journey with Palawi, owing to her work and for standing up for the rights of HIV+ kids. She was driven out of schools when she tried to get admissions for her children. “The discrimination against our children was such that they were not even allowed to set foot in any of the schools in this area. We were left with no choice but to start a school of our own,” says Mangaltai.The healing power of loveAccording to Mangaltai, the love that children of Palawi receive is one of the strongest factors in their recovery and healthy childhood. “We are there for these kids throughout the day. These kids have low immunity and are prone to illness. Which is why we need to be extremely cautious. We need to have regular health check-ups to ensure they are safe and healthy. But all of this comes at a cost. A cost that I alone can not take care of,” Mangaltai says. Help Mangaltai change the lives of hundreds of abandoned HIV+ orphans and give them a loving home, medical care and education, so they too can live fulfilling lives in future. Donate now.

campaigns.title: Pledge to rebuild Punjab, support the state’s children and their education
campaigns.description: Punjab was once widely recognised as a land of abundance and promise. Unfortunately, the state today is staring at a crisis in the making. And this time, the most vulnerable of all, the children of Punjab, are at the receiving end.Onground reports suggest a large number of children fail to complete schooling, and some others may be completely out of the education system.But our NGO partner, Sanjhi Sikhiya, has been at the forefront, fighting for the children of Punjab and ensuring their right to an education and a future ahead. But sadly, the NGO and its young founders can't do this alone. Your donation can fuel the transformation that Punjab needs. Donate generously!Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).A crisis at the gatesPunjab's children are facing an unprecedented education crisis that threatens their future and the state's prosperity. One in four young people in Punjab feels there's no future for them in their own state. The opportunities they see around them don't match their hopes and aspirations.The impact on the groundStarted by a group of well-educated youths, Sanjhi Sakhiya has already begun to make its presence felt and bring about visible change. In just a few years, Sanjhi Sikhiya has reached 1,200 schools across Punjab, trained and supported 5,000 teachers, impacted 1.22 lakh students, and empowered 94 youth leaders who are driving change in their communities.Real change happens when communities take ownership. The NGO has mobilised over 8,000 community members who refuse to accept mediocre education for their children. They have empowered 516 School Management Committees to move from paperwork to real action, and created 584 Teacher Collectives where educators support and learn from each other.When Punjab faced its toughest test, Sanjhi Sikhiya empowered youth to restore safe and joyful spaces for every child, covering 100 schools, turning flooded classrooms into Schools of Strength.What your support will doYour donations contribute to expanding this movement. Your donation has the power to reignite the spark of learning. It will provide materials that make abstract concepts concrete for children struggling with the basics. It will support teachers who want to do better but need resources and training to make it happen.This isn't charity. This is an investment in Punjab's future. Join Punjab's Transformation. Donate nowYour support will help the NGO reach more districts, more schools, more children who are currently sitting in classrooms where hope is in short supply.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Sanjhi Sikhiya, in its mission to give children of Punjab the future they deserve.

campaigns.title: Help Satya's kids with special needs
campaigns.description:
Children with special needs do not receive appropriate individualized therapy and education in Pondicherry. Their development is further delayed without proper therapy. Children with special needs who can lead independent lives as contributing members of society are isolated and their possible growth is stunted. In rural Pondicherry, there are many superstitions surrounding disability, especially intellectual and developmental disabilities.  
 Satya Special School's programs combine modern, traditional, and alternative therapies for optimal development of each child with special needs. With a trained team of special educators, physiotherapists, speech therapists, occupational therapists, and others such as musicians, artists, and performers, Satya's programs help multiply the effect of each kind of intervention.We provide children with the following therapies and resources for development: Special educationPhysiotherapyOccupational TherapySpeech TherapySensory IntegrationHydrotherapyAquatherapyMusic therapyArt therapyDrama therapyHobbiesVocational trainingPlacement and any resource that will help the child and/or the parents access a better life for the child. Many of the children at our centres go on to be integrated into mainstream schools, and some of them move on to our vocational centres for training.We have around 1100 children across our ten centres, including three village centres. While some of the projects are funded, we need help for about 50 children urgently.  
 We wish to provide therapy that will enable the 50 children to reach their maximum potential: be it performing their own Activities of Daily Living, graduating Class 10 under National Open Schooling, or moving on to study a vocation at the community college.We want each child to receive all the resources necessary to enable their maximum development. We wish to mainstream at least 5 of these 50 children in government or private schools.

campaigns.title: Adoption of 62 Widow Ladies from Eraiyur village for Daily Support
campaigns.description:
Eraiyur village, (Kanchipuram district, close to Sriperumbudur), is a remote village about 44 km south-west of the city of Chennai. Here, a set of old village ladies are suffering, having no kith and kin or any income from any quarter except owning a small house in the village. Most of them are above 75 years of age and their day to day living is becoming problematic.  These elderly senior citizens can no more cook for themselves due to advanced age and slowly in the 3 years that we have been supporting them, their number has grown and at present, there are 62 such ladies in Eraiyur village who are in need of help and support 
 About 3 years ago, NLCT started supporting 10 widow ladies and has now expanded support to about 62 ladies. NLCT now provides a packet of groceries to each of the 62 elderly ladies every month and also takes care of their medical expenses. Those who cannot cook, hand over these groceries to their neighbours who cook for them along with cooking for their families.Apart from these, in case of any urgent needs, these ladies approach NLCT home for help. This way, their day to day problems are taken care and they are able to handle their lives better.Presently due to meagre corpus NLCT is unable to fulfil the recurring expenses of both 120 residents of NLCT home along with 62 community senior citizens family, however the present cost of each senior citizen's monthly requirement tunes to Rs.2500/- per person. 
 With NLCT supporting them, we are confident that they are living a better life as they don’t have to struggle for their daily needs and don’t have to starve. As they can no more work for themselves for their livelihood, this support is very helpful. NLCT is not in a position to in-house all the 62 senior citizens due to space constraints, but we found a solution by offering to help them with groceries, medicines, medical help/camps, ambulance services etc, so that they can live in their own homes, but get all other support forms NLCT.

campaigns.title: Help abandoned elderly with shelter and care
campaigns.description: Every day, elderly parents are left alone on the streets, at train stations, or even outside temples. Many are sick, struggling with age-related problems, and have no money or family to turn to. Some suffer for weeks or months before anyone notices their pain. The heartbreaking truth? They were abandoned by their own families.Although Indian law requires children and relatives to care for senior citizens, many elders still face neglect, abuse, and abandonment. The problem is only getting worse.But there is hope. Our dedicated NGO partners work day and night to rescue these forgotten elders. They provide safe shelter, medical care, nutritious meals, and most importantly—love and respect. Through old age homes across the country, our partners give these seniors a chance to live their remaining years with dignity, comfort, and peace.You can make a difference. Your donation will help our NGO partners:Rescue homeless elders from the streetsProvide shelter, food, and medical careRestore their faith in humanity with kindness and supportNo elder should spend their final years alone and suffering. Your generosity can give them a fresh start. Donate today and help an abandoned senior citizen find safety, care, and hope.

campaigns.title: Sponsor monthly groceries for the elderly
campaigns.description:
The population of senior citizens is increasing as life expectancy is increasing. Families in urban areas are becoming nuclear and smaller, as a result of which there are fewer people available in the house to provide care and comfort to the ageing.Due to the increase in number of the elderly population their needs and problems are increasing too. There are many senior citizens who are without any financial support and are living below the poverty line. There are senior citizens with small pensions but most of it goes for medical treatment and hardly anything is left for nutritional support. For such needy senior citizens, Dignity Foundation has started Ration Service. The needy elderly are given dry rations for sustenance every month. The senior citizens are then tracked through home visits.The elderly deserve dignity in their last days and a little support can help them with the dignity they deserve. 
 Dignity foundation ration service’s main aim is to provide at least one day’s full meal to elderly person or couple for full month. Dignity foundation with the help of our volunteers conduct camps or surveys in Slum areas of Mumbai and find out elderly person or couple, who are suffering from food storage due to financial in balance. Ration service is provided, if that elderly person or couple fits to beneficiary criteria. Our monthly ration service consider of:- Atta–5kg,Rice–3kg,Sugar–1kg,Tur dal–1kg,Tea–250gm,Cooking Oil–2ltr, Moong full 500gm, Salt–1kg,Poha–500gm,Rawa–1kg 
 Dignity Foundation wants to evaluate the results of its programs by assessing the increase in various indicators, such as increase in nutrition levels (based on self reported daily calorie consumption), or by improvements in medically assessed indicators (like weight, weight-to-age, weight-to-height ratios, CBC indicators etc).

campaigns.description: I am fundraising for a cause close to my heart, to bring a lasting change in the lives of the underprivileged. To help me create more impact and touch more lives, I need your support and contribution. Please back my fundraiser so that together we can build a better India for all of us.
